steps.title: Use the comparison
steps.content: To refute the claim that apricots are a better vitamin C source, the answer must compare the vitamin C amounts directly. The notes show kale has 120 mg per 100 g, while apricot has only 10 mg per 100 g.

steps.title: Reject incomplete uses
steps.content: Choice A gives kale's amount but never compares it with apricots. Choice B says both foods contain vitamin C without showing which has more. Choice C explains that humans need vitamin C, not which food is the better source. The answer is D.
